2605.09271.title: Shaping Schema via Language Representation as the Next Frontier for LLM Intelligence Expanding
2605.09271.paper_summary: Although natural language is the default medium for Large Language Models (LLMs), its limited expressive capacity creates a profound bottleneck for complex problem-solving. While recent advancements in AI have relied heavily on scaling, merely internalizing knowledge does not guarantee its effective application. Defining language representation as the linguistic and symbolic constructs used to map and model the real world, this paper argues that shaping schemas through advanced language representation is the next frontier for expanding LLM intelligence. We posit that an LLM's knowledge activation and organization -- its schema -- depends heavily on the structural and symbolic sophistication of the language used to represent a given task. This paper contributes both a formalization of this claim and the empirical evidence to support it. With a new formalization, we present multiple lines of evidence to support our position: Firstly, we review recent empirical practices and emerging methodologies that demonstrate the substantial performance gains achievable through deliberate language representation design, even without modifying model parameters or scale. Secondly, we conduct controlled experiments showing that LLM performance and its internal feature activations vary under different language representations of the same underlying task. Together, these findings highlight language representation design as a promising direction for future research.
2605.09271.paper_summary_zh: 雖然自然語言是大型語言模型（LLMs）的默認媒介，但其有限的表達能力對於複雜問題解決造成了深刻的瓶頸。儘管近期在人工智慧方面的進展在很大程度上依賴於擴展，但僅僅內化知識並不能保證其有效應用。本文將語言表徵定義為用於映射和建模現實世界的語言和符號構造，並主張通過先進的語言表徵塑造框架是擴展LLM智慧的下一個前沿。我們認為，LLM的知識激活和組織——其框架——在很大程度上取決於用於表徵特定任務的語言的結構和符號的複雜性。本文不僅對這一主張進行了形式化，還提供了實證證據來支持它。通過新的形式化，我們提出了多條證據來支持我們的觀點：首先，我們回顧了近期的實證實踐和新興方法論，這些方法論展示了通過有意的語言表徵設計，即使不修改模型參數或規模，仍然可以實現可觀的性能提升。其次，我們進行了受控實驗，顯示LLM的性能及其內部特徵激活在相同基礎任務的不同語言表徵下存在變化。綜合這些發現，語言表徵設計被突顯為未來研究的一個有前景的方向。
2605.09271.author: Zhiqin Yang et.al.
2605.09271.authors: Zhiqin Yang, Yuhan Liu, Jingwen Fu, Pei Fu anf Bo Han, Masashi Sugiyama, Nanning Zheng
